headline: Is It Too Late To Consider J.B. Hunt (JBHT) After Its 69% One Year Rally?
summary: If you are wondering whether J.B. Hunt Transport Services at around US$225 a share still offers value, the key question is how the current price stacks up against a range of valuation checks. The stock closed at US$225.22, with reported returns of 4.8% over 7 days, 4.3% over 30 days, 14.5% year to date, 69.5% over 1 year, 32.9% over 3 years and 38.9% over 5 years, which naturally raises questions about how much of the story is already reflected in the price. Recent coverage of J.B. Hunt...

headline: J.B. Hunt to Report Q1 Earnings: What's in Store for the Stock?
summary: JBHT's first-quarter 2026 revenues are expected to have improved year over year. Higher net interest expense might have hurt the bottom line.

headline: Should J.B. Hunt’s (JBHT) Early Use of a Heavy-Duty EV Charging Hub Require Action From Investors?
summary: EV Realty has opened a commercial electric vehicle charging site in Southern California with 76 stalls and 9 megawatts of capacity, and J.B. Hunt Transport Services is among the initial customers using the facility near the San Bernardino Intermodal Facility. This move underlines J.B. Hunt’s early participation in heavy-duty fleet electrification infrastructure, potentially reinforcing its role in lower-emission freight transportation solutions. We’ll now examine how J.B. headline: Exploring Analyst Estimates for JB Hunt (JBHT) Q1 Earnings, Beyond Revenue and EPS
summary: Get a deeper insight into the potential performance of JB Hunt (JBHT) for the quarter ended March 2026 by going beyond Wall Street's top-and-bottom-line estimates and examining the estimates for some of its key metrics.
